title: item_p0015
complex: Such is the lot of the modern-day chemist: you wait ages for a new element to turn up and then four come along at once.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: Discovered by researchers in Japan, Russia and the US, the four new elements are the first to be added to the periodic table since 2011, when elements 114 and 116 were included.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: The new elements, all spectacularly short-lived and highly radioactive, complete the periodic table’s seventh row and render science textbooks around the world out of date.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: The US-based International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry (IUPAC), the global organization that governs chemical nomenclature, terminology and measurement, verified the elements on 30 December, 2015 after poring over studies dating back to 2004.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: The scientists who found them must now come up with formal names to replace the clunky Latinbased placeholders – ununtrium, ununpentium, ununseptium and ununoctium – which reflect their atomic numbers, 113, 115, 117, and 118.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: The atomic number is the number of protons found in an element’s atomic nucleus.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: IUPAC announced that a Russian-American team of scientists at the Joint Institute for Nuclear Research in Dubna and Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory in California had produced sufficient evidence to claim the discovery of elements 115, 117 and 118.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: The body awarded credit for the discovery of element 113, which had also been claimed by the Russians and Americans, to a team of scientists from the RIKEN Institute in Japan.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: The decision means Japan becomes the first Asian country to name an element.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: Under IUPAC rules, new elements can be named after mythological concepts, minerals, a place or country, or a scientist.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: When elements 114 and 116 were assigned formal names in 2012, scientists chose flerovium and livermorium respectively, after the Flerov Lab at Dubna’s Joint Institute of Research and the Lawrence Livermore Lab in the US, where the elements were discovered.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: Kosuke Morita, who led the research at RIKEN, said his team now planned to “look to the uncharted territory of element 119 and beyond.” Jan Reedijk, president of the Inorganic Chemistry Division of IUPAC, said: “The chemistry community is eager to see its most cherished table finally being completed down to the seventh row.” The Japanese team is believed to be considering three names for ununtrium: japonium, rikenium and nishinarium, after the Nishina Center for Accelerator-Based Science, where the element was found.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: “They will have been thinking about it for a while already,” said Polly Arnold, professor of chemistry at Edinburgh University.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: “This is painstaking work.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: All this trying to understand Mother Nature helps us with our models and with understanding radioactive decay.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: If we understand it better, hopefully we can do better at dealing with nuclear waste and things that are important in the real world.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: It also leads to fantastic technological advances in building the kit to make these observations.” Along with new names, the scientists must propose two-letter symbols for the elements.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: When IUPAC has received the researchers’ suggestions, they will be put up for public review for five months.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: That allows scientists and others to raise any objections.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: In 1996, the symbol Cp was proposed for copernicium, or element 112, but it was swapped to Cn when scientists complained that Cp referred to another substance.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: To discover the elements, researchers at the three labs slammed lighter nuclei into one another and looked for signature radioactive decays that should come from the new elements.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: Ununtrium and ununpentium are thought to be metals, while ununseptium could be a metalloid – a material bearing some metallic properties.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: The fourth element, ununoctium, may be a noble gas, like other group-18 elements, helium, neon and argon.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: It is hard to know for sure because so few atoms of each element have ever been made.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: Paul Karol, chair of the IUPAC panel that verified the elements, said: “For now, most of the successes will be used by nuclear theorists to improve their understanding of the structure and stability of these very heavy nuclei as experimenters seek the alleged but highly probable ‘island of stability’ at or near element 120 or perhaps 126.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: It might be that those elements have long enough lifetimes for their detailed chemistry to be explored.
label: ignore

title: item_p0015
complex: Practical applications of the new elements, if any, are a long way off because of the difficulty in synthesis.”

title: item_p0016
complex: Brazil’s latest funk sensation, Anitta, has won millions of fans by taking the favela sound into the mainstream, but she is at the centre of a debate about skin colour.

title: item_p0016
complex: Anti-discrimination campaigners and social commentators say the music industry’s fastest rising star has had to sacrifice her blackness to make it into the predominantly white middleclass market.

title: item_p0016
complex: The controversy was prompted by the publication of then-and-now photographs that show a dramatic lightening of Anitta’s skin tone since she signed a deal with Warner.
label: ignore

title: item_p0016
complex: In the first, when she was relatively unknown, she looked darker.

title: item_p0016
complex: In the second – a marketing shot after she became famous – she seems paler.

title: item_p0016
complex: Whether this was the result of whitening products and cosmetic surgery or – more likely – Photoshop tweaks, the contrast has rekindled discussion about whether you need to be pale to get ahead in Brazil.

title: item_p0016

complex: In her teens, she made a name for herself in Rio de Janeiro’s baile funk scene as a dancer and singer.

title: item_p0016
complex: Though adored first and foremost as a pop idol with a strong message and some catchy tunes, her backers project her as a cultural bridge between the predominantly black and mixed-race shanty towns on Rio’s hillsides and the wealthier and whiter communities below.

title: item_p0016
complex: She has toned down the suggestive dancing, gangsta references and explicit lyrics of baile funk.

complex: Now, however, questions are being asked about whether she – or her marketing team – have gone too far in re-tailoring her image to attract a more lucrative demographic.

complex: “If pop stars have curly hair, they are going to feel coerced into straightening it.
label: ignore

complex: If they have a big nose, they will be coerced into getting rhinoplasty,” said Arraes.
label: ignore

title: item_p0016
complex: “It creates a vicious cycle for self-esteem.” This is a sensitive topic in this largely mixed-raced nation.
label: ignore

title: item_p0016
complex: Brazil – one of the last big countries in the world to ban slavery – has the largest population of African descent outside Africa, but race and ancestry are less important here than colour.

title: item_p0016
complex: And, despite the nation’s goal of being a multiracial democracy, there is a clear link between skin tone and inequality.

complex: Up until 2011, black or mixed-race students also spent two years less at school on average.

label: ignore

title: item_p0016
complex: But the gulf remains glaringly apparent.

label: ignore

title: item_p0016
complex: Walk through Ipanema, Gávea or other upmarket districts and you are far more likely to see black nannies pushing strollers with white toddlers than a white nanny pushing a black child.

title: item_p0016
complex: Defining colour is complex.

complex: People who define themselves as white were in the minority for the first time in the most recent census in 2010.

title: item_p0016
complex: Among the 197 million population, 82 million said they were “pardu” (mixed race), 15 million black, two million Asian and 0.5% indigenous.

title: item_p0016
complex: Sylvio Ferreira, a psychology lecturer at the Federal University of Pernambuco, believes Anitta has won the hearts of the middle class by taking a rebellious sound and making it tamer and more palatable to everyone.

complex: “Was this achieved by racial whitening?

complex: No,” he said.
label: ignore

complex: “What happened was a change of the social space where Anitta produced her art: from the periphery to the centre.” Others agree that the issue of colour is overblown.

complex: Maycon de Mattos Batista, a financial analyst who worked with Anitta while she was an intern, said there had been a huge change in Anitta’s image, but not of her colour.

title: item_p0016
complex: “I don’t believe it’s whitening; it’s more the way they are producing her with makeup, hairstylists and the way she dresses,” he said.

title: item_p0016
complex: “I don’t think that was because of pressure being put on her.
label: ignore

title: item_p0016
complex: She always liked to show off, sing and dance.

title: item_p0016
complex: That was a natural thing for her.
label: ignore

title: item_p0016
complex: I believe that it is because of this naturalness that she is where she is today.” Leandro Silva de Souza, a racial equality activist in the north-eastern city of Salvador, said the prejudice lay not with society but with music producers and media executives.

complex: The regulation eight hours in the office is over.

title: item_p0018

complex: This is the point many workers would think about heading for the door.

title: item_p0018
complex: But, after decades of giving companies carte blanche to milk every last drop of productivity from their workforce, a challenge to Japan’s ingrained culture of overwork has come from the government, which is considering making it a legal requirement for workers to take at least five days’ paid holiday a year.

title: item_p0018
complex: Japanese employees are currently entitled to an average 18.5 days’ paid holiday a year – only two fewer than the global average – with a minimum of ten days, as well as 15 one-day national holidays.

title: item_p0018

title: item_p0018

title: item_p0018

complex: I let out a tiny breath and this helps.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: Eventually, I give in, expelling the spent air in my lungs and taking a huge gasp.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: (And continue to gasp for a few more breaths, prompting my husband to ask what on earth IÕm doing.)
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: I manage one minute and 12 seconds.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: IÕm quite impressed with myself.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: Breath-holding ability becomes extremely important in some sports, particularly freediving.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: In 2006, I was lming a programme about the anatomy and physiology of the lungs for a BBC series called, slightly oddly, DonÕt Die Young.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: I was lucky enough to meet Sam Amps, who was captain of the UK freedive team.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: At a pool in Bristol, she taught me some simple exercises to help me hold my breath for longer while swimming underwater.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: By the end of the session, I think IÕd managed a prodigious 90 seconds of breath-holding, enough to let me swim a width.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: Sam swam three widths with ease.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: She could hold her breath for ve minutes, while swimming.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: Five!
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: I asked how she did it: very slow breathing for several minutes prior to each dive, then a big, deep breath before diving in.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: She also said training helped her resist the urge to breathe for far longer than most people.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: Some have suggested that the ability to voluntarily hold your breath is evidence of a watery episode in human evolution.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: ItÕs even been said that humans have an ability to lower heart rate and metabolic rate in order to breath-hold for even longer.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: Other anatomical and physiological bits and bobs Ð our hairlessness, the distribution of our subcutaneous fat and even our tendency to walk on two legs Ð have been linked to an aquatic phase of evolutionary development.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: Unfortunately, the cobbled-together Òaquatic ape hypothesisÓ fails to hold water.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: ItÕs a romantic notion that may appeal to us but, with the cold light of day falling on the scienti c evidence, itÕs revealed to be nothing more than a fiction.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: Looking at voluntary breath-holding, it turns out that weÕre certainly not unique among non-aquatic mammals in being able to hold our breath.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: (Having said that, itÕs a dif cult thing to investigate in other mammals as, unlike humans, they tend not to comply when you ask them to breath-hold.)
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: And experimental evidence shows that heart rate doesnÕt drop during breath-holding.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: At least, it doesnÕt if youÕre breath-holding on land.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: When youÕre submerged in cold water itÕs a different story: cooling the face does lead to a slower heart rate in most people.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: But, once again, this isnÕt evidence of an aquatic ape ancestry, as it turns out to be a very general characteristic of air-breathing vertebrates.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: This reduction in heart rate is just one of the physiological responses that are sometimes described together as the Òmammalian diving re exÓ.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: But physiological responses that could be useful in diving are also Ð and, perhaps, even more importantly Ð useful for not drowning.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: While our ability to breath-hold may not be all that special, when we compare ourselves with other animals, itÕs now proving very useful in one particular area of medicine.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: Radiotherapy for breast cancer involves directing radiation, very precisely, at the tumour.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: This may require several minutesÕ worth of radiation and, so, itÕs usually done in short bursts, between breaths.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: But, if the patient can keep her chest perfectly still for several minutes, it means that the entire dose can be delivered, in the right place, in one go.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: The problem, of course, is that most people, just like me, struggle to hold their breath for much longer than a minute.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: But doctors at University Hospital Birmingham have recently performed careful experiments that show that, if patients are ventilated with oxygen-rich air before attempting a breath- hold, they can manage to hold their breath for an impressive ve-and-a-half minutes.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: Surprisingly, the trick seems to lie not in fooling the bodyÕs usual sensors for low oxygen or high carbon dioxide levels in the blood but in fooling the diaphragm.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: When you breathe in, youÕre contracting the muscle of your diaphragm, pulling it at so that the volume of your chest increases Ð and air is drawn into your lungs.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: When you hold your breath, you keep your diaphragm in this contracted state.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: Arti cially raising oxygen levels and reducing carbon dioxide levels before a breath-hold, as in the Birmingham radiotherapy experiments, may work by delaying fatigue in the diaphragm.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: And Ð not so useful if youÕre trying to keep your chest perfectly still Ð breathing out a little air lets the diaphragm relax a little, and helps you to prolong a breath-hold, exactly as I found when attempting my breath-hold.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: And, so, itÕs your diaphragm, the main muscle of breathing, that is also in charge when it comes to reaching the breakpoint of your breath-hold.
label: ignore

title: item_p0019
complex: Eventually, even if youÕve fooled it for a while, the signals from the diaphragm are just too strong and you have to give in Ð and take a breath.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: Nobody knows which came first: the economic crisis tearing Greece apart or shisha, the drug now known as the “cocaine of the poor”.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: What everyone does accept is that shisha is a killer; and at €2 or less a hit, it is one that has come to stalk Greece, the country long on the frontline of Europe’s financial meltdown.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: “As drugs go, it is the worst.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: It burns your insides, it makes you aggressive and ensures that you go totally mad,” said Maria, a former heroin addict.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: “But it is cheap and it is easy to get, and it is what everyone is doing.” This drug crisis has put Athens’s health authorities, already overwhelmed by draconian cuts, under further strain.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: The drug of preference for thousands of homeless Greeks forced on to the streets by poverty and despair, shisha is described by both addicts and officials as a variant of crystal meth, whose potential to send users into a state of mindless violence is underpinned by the substances with which the synthetic drug is frequently mixed: battery acid, engine oil and even shampoo.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: Worse still, it is not only readily available but easy to make – tailor-made for a society that sees little light at the end of the tunnel.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: “It is a killer, but it also makes you want to kill,” said Konstantinos, a drug addict.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: “You can kill without understanding that you have done it.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: And it is spreading faster than death.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: A lot of users have died.” For Charalampos Poulopoulos, the head of Kethea, Greece’s pre-eminent anti-drug centre, shisha symbolizes the depredations of a crisis that has led to record levels of destitution and unemployment.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: It is, he said, an “austerity drug” – the response of dealers who have become ever more adept at producing synthetic drugs designed for those who can no longer afford more expensive highs from such drugs as heroin and cocaine.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: “The crisis has given dealers the possibility to promote a new, cheap drug, a cocaine for the poor,” said Poulopoulos at a centre run for addicts in Exarcheia, the anarchist stronghold in Athens.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: “Shisha can be sniffed or injected and it can be made in home laboratories – you don’t need any specialized knowledge.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: It is extremely dangerous.” Across Greece, the byproducts of six straight years of recession have been brutal and cruel.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: Depression, along with drug and alcohol abuse, has risen dramatically.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: Delinquency and crime have soared as Greek society has unravelled under the weight of austerity measures that have cut the income of ordinary Greeks by 40%.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: Prostitution – the easiest way of financing drug addiction – has similarly skyrocketed.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: “Desperation is such that many women agree to engage in unprotected sex because that way they’ll make more money,” said Eleni Marini, a British-trained psychologist with Kethea.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: “Shisha has been linked to a very intense sexual drive but it attacks your ability to think straight and we’re seeing a lot more pregnancies among drug addicts who engage in prostitution.” In 2012, two sex workers gave birth on the streets of Athens.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: At a time when suicides have also shot up and the spread of HIV infections has assumed epidemic proportions, drug addicts (a population believed to be around 25,000 strong) have become increasingly self-destructive.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: And, experts say, young Greeks marginalized by record rates of unemployment – at 64% Greece has the highest youth unemployment in the EU – are leading the way.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: “The crisis has created a widespread sense of pessimism,” said Poulopoulos.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: “For those who might have quit drugs, there is now no incentive.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: Instead, there’s an atmosphere of misery, where people knowing they won’t find work are becoming a lot more self-destructive.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: In Athens, where the economic crisis has hit hardest, shisha is part of that.” Greece’s conservative-dominated coalition government has tried to deal with the problem by driving drug users and other homeless people out of the city centre – a series of controversial police operations has swept central streets, clearing crowded doorways and malls.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: “But with such actions, authorities are only sweeping the problem under the carpet,” said Poulopoulos.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: “What, in reality, they are really doing is marginalizing these people even more by pushing them into the arms of drug dealers who offer them protection.” Just when the demand for help has never been greater, state-funded organizations such as Kethea have had their budgets slashed by a third at the request of the “troika” – the EC, ECB and IMF – keeping the debt-stricken Greek economy afloat.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: Since the outbreak of the crisis in 2009, Kethea has lost 70 of its 500 staff.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: The cuts come despite studies showing that, for every euro invested in programmes such as Kethea, the state saves about €6 in costs to the criminal justice and healthcare systems.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: “The cuts we have witnessed are a false economy, a huge mistake,” said Poulopoulos.
label: ignore

title: item_p0020
complex: On the streets of Athens, the breeding ground of shisha, there is rising fear that austerity not only doesn’t work – it kills.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: It began with a bogus scallop, but a menu scandal that has engulfed some of Japan’s most prestigious hotels and department stores now threatens to undermine the international reputation of the country’s vaunted cuisine.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: Since one luxury hotel chain admitted lying about the provenance of ingredients on its menus, Japanese media have served up almost daily revelations of similar transgressions by restaurants run by well-known hotels and department stores.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: The frenzy began when the Hankyu-Hanshin hotel chain, based in Osaka, admitted it had given false descriptions of dozens of menu items at some of its restaurants between 2006 and October 2013, affecting an estimated 78,000 diners.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: Among the chief menu misdemeanours was a red salmon ‘caviar’ dish that turned out to be the less sumptuous eggs of the flying fish.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: A televised attempt by the hotel group’s president, Hiroshi Desaki, to limit the damage by announcing a 20% pay cut for himself and 10% for other executives, failed to mollify angry consumers.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: Days later, Desaki resigned, conceding that the hotel group had “betrayed our customers”, although he added: “We never had the intention to deceive them.” One of the hotel’s head chefs later declined a medal of honour he was due to receive from the government.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: The company has so far refunded more than 10,000 consumers to the tune of 20m yen; the eventual bill is expected to reach 110m yen.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: Japan’s version of the UK horsemeat scandal has since spread to several household names in catering.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: While, as in the UK, no one has fallen ill from eating mislabelled produce, the outbreak of anger shows no sign of abating.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: Consumers who believed they had eaten prized kuruma shrimps, for example, were told they had in fact dined on the much cheaper black tiger version.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: The first incident went almost unnoticed.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: The Prince Hotel in Tokyo was forced to come clean after a diner complained in a blogpost that a ‘scallop’ dish he had ordered contained a similar, but cheaper, type of shellfish.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: The hotel launched an investigation and went on to correct more than 50 menu items at dozens of its restaurants.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: Its report scared Hankyu-Hanshin and other hoteliers into admitting that they, too, had hoodwinked diners who believed they were paying high prices for premium ingredients.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: The Hotel Okura chain – whose guests have included Barack Obama – confessed myriad sins, including injecting beef with fat to make it juicier and incorrectly describing tomatoes as organic.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: “We deeply apologize for betraying the expectations and confidence of our clients,” it said in a statement.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: The list of fraudulent ingredients continues to grow: orange juice from cartons sold as freshly squeezed; Mont Blanc desserts topped with Korean chestnuts instead of the promised French ones; bought-in chocolate cream masquerading as home-made; imported beef sold as high-end wagyu.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: Even the government’s top spokesman, Yoshihide Suga, was moved to comment on the scandal.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: “These incidents have surfaced one after the other and this inappropriate labelling has resulted in the loss of trust among consumers,” he told reporters.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: “These are clearly cover-ups.” The fraudulent menu scandal has exploded at just the wrong time.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: Japan is trying to persuade South Korea and other countries to lift bans on food imports imposed in response to the Fukushima nuclear accident, while UNESCO is considering a request to add Japanese cuisine to its intangible cultural heritage list.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: One local newspaper ran the headline, “Japan’s proud food culture in tears,” while the mass circulation Yomiuri Shimbun said it was “astonished by the industry’s lack of morals.” The newspaper voiced concerns that the scandal could “harm the credibility of brand Japan, products and services, which are praised by foreign countries and tourists for their safety and security.” Industry experts said the global financial crisis in 2008 had forced luxury hotels to cut costs while attempting to woo diners with detailed menu descriptions “Menu descriptions were created to meet consumers’ preference for brand products, and, when they couldn’t obtain the ingredients stated on the menu, hotels just used food from different places of origin,” Hiroshi Tomozawa, a hotel and restaurant consultant, told Kyodo News.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: While they count the cost to their reputations, the hotels and restaurants involved are unlikely to face legal action.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: Menus are not covered by the agricultural standards law or by a new food labelling law due to go into effect in 2015.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: The authorities’ only legal weapon is a law banning misleading representations of goods and services.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: The industry’s biggest nemesis will be Japan’s discerning and demanding consumers.
label: ignore

title: item_p0021
complex: In a 2009 poll conducted by an online restaurant guide, 72% of respondents said provenance was the most important factor in selecting dishes from a menu, followed by calorific and nutritional details.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: Young Cubans are flocking to use the first known free, open-access internet service in the communist island nation, which has been made possible by one of Cuba’s most famous artists.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: A modest cultural centre in the capital city, Havana, has suddenly become a rare source of free wi-fi.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: The internationally renowned Cuban artist Kcho is providing the service.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: Perhaps more surprisingly, his spokesman said the move had been approved by the state-owned telecommunications utility, Etecsa.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: The service is reportedly very slow, especially when the centre gets crowded.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: But, in a country where only an estimated 5% of the population has unrestricted access to the internet, a facility that is both free of charge and free of restrictions is being hailed as an unprecedented boon.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: The chance to click on international news websites, communicate with friends and family overseas and use sites like Facebook and Twitter has created a massive buzz.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: “I come as often as I can,” said Adonis Ortiz, 20, while video-chatting with his father, who lives in the US and whom he has not seen in nine years.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: The gradual loosening of the long diplomatic and trade freeze between the US and Cuba is expected to bring American tech giants such as Google and Apple into the Cuban market as soon as they are permitted.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: In the meantime, Cuba has installed a high-speed, fibre-optic cable under the sea from Venezuela and internet users have some access to Chinese equipment.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: Another estimate, that a quarter of Cubans have access to the internet – still one of the lowest penetration rates in the Western Hemisphere – in fact measures residents who use a restricted domestic intranet featuring only certain websites and limited email.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: Kcho has offered the public admission to his own personal internet connection.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: But, instead of this being the counter-revolutionary, or post-revolutionary, move of a free-market rebel, Kcho may have been selected as the acceptable face of a regime acquiescing to the inevitable lure of the internet.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: Kcho, who has close ties to the Cuban government, announced that his actions had been approved by the Ministry of Culture.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: The artist said he wanted to encourage Cubans to familiarize themselves with the internet.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: “It’s only possible through the will to do it and absorb the costs,” Kcho told the Associated Press.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: “It is expensive but the benefit is tremendous.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: I have something that is great and powerful.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: I can share it and I am doing so.” Kcho’s real name is Alexis Leiva Machado.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: He gained international fame for his painting, sculpture and drawings after winning the grand prize at a prominent art biennial in South Korea.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: He is currently preparing for the Havana biennial in May.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: Born on one of Cuba’s outlying islands, he is known for contemporary art with rustic, seaside and patriotic themes and imagery.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: In the centre’s courtyard, tech-savvy millennials lounge throughout the day in wicker chairs beneath a white canopy or just outside when it’s packed, tapping away on laptops and tablets or glued to their smartphones.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: Cuba has some of the lowest connectivity rates on the planet, with dial-up accounts closely restricted and at-home broadband almost unheard of except in the case of foreigners who pay hundreds of dollars a month for the service in a country where the average salary is between $17 and $20 a month.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: Kcho is estimated to be paying $900 a month to provide the free wi-fi.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: Since 2013, Cuban authorities have opened hundreds of internet salons, where an hour online costs $4.50, at speeds far lower than those at Kcho’s studio of around 2mbps.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: A 2014 report by Akamai Technologies found average internet connectivity speeds to be around 10.5mbps in the US and 23.6mbps in world-leading South Korea.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: Globally, the average was about 3.9mbps.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: With dozens of users at any given time, the signal strength of Kcho’s wi-fi gets diluted.
label: ignore

title: item_p0022
complex: One user said he sometimes swings by in the middle of the night, when nobody else is around, and finds it to be unbelievably fast.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: A nasal spray laced with the ‘Love hormone’ oxytocin could help children with autism learn to handle social situations better, US researchers claim.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: Scans of children with autistic spectrum disorder showed that a single dose of the chemical improved brain responses to facial expressions, a shift that could make social interactions feel more natural and rewarding for them.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: The scientists behind the research said a course of oxytocin might boost the success of behavioural therapies that are already used to help people with autism learn to cope with social situations.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: “Over time, what you would expect to see is more appropriate social responding, being more interested in interacting with other people, more eye contact and more conversational ability,” said Kevin Pelphrey, director of the Child Neuroscience Lab at Yale University.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: Autism is a developmental disorder seen in more than one in 100 people.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: The condition affects individuals in different ways, but is characterized by difficulties in social interaction and communication.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: So far, there is no established treatment for the social problems caused by autism.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: Researchers at Yale have studied the brain chemical oxytocin as a potential treatment for the social impairments caused by autism because it plays a crucial role in bonding and trust.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: Results have been mixed, though: one recent study found no significant benefit for youths given the chemical over several days.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: But Pelphrey said oxytocin might help the brain learn from social interactions; it would work best when used with therapies that encourage people with autism to engage more socially, he said.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: “Our study shows that oxytocin affects the brain and opens up the possibility that, when combined with behavioural treatments, it works like a social enhancer,” he said.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: The scientists used a technique called functional MRI to scan the brains of 17 youths aged eight to 16 with autism while they looked at images of cars or the eyes of people expressing various emotions.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: The scans were given 45 minutes after the participants inhaled a placebo or oxytocin through a nasal spray.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: The scans showed that reward circuitry in the children’s brains behaved more normally after a snort of oxytocin, being more active when the person was looking at faces and less active when viewing the inanimate cars.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: The study appears in the latest issue of the journal Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences in the United States.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: “If this is replicated, it suggests that oxytocin might treat something for which we don’t have a treatment in autism, and that’s the core social motivation,” Pelphrey told the Guardian.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: He warned that it was too early to use oxytocin as a treatment for the social difficulties caused by autism and cautioned against buying oxytocin from suppliers online.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: “We don’t want them running out on the basis of this study or any other and trying oxytocin at home.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: There is no telling what they are buying.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: We are nowhere near thinking this is a ready treatment.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: It needs more follow-up,” he said.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: “This is an important new study in identifying changes in brain activity in key regions of the brain involved in social cognition in autism following oxytocin administration,” said Simon Baron-Cohen, director of the Autism Research Centre at Cambridge University.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: A surprising finding, however, is that oxytocin nasal spray did not change performance on the social cognitive task.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: Nor is it clear yet if oxytocin only has benefits for people with autism or has any unwanted side effects.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: Finally, oxytocin effects only last about 45 minutes, so there may be practical considerations as to whether this could be used as a treatment.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: “From a scientific perspective, this study has a lot of evidence from animal and human work to justify serious attention, but more research is needed.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: Doctors should be cautious about the clinical potential of this hormone until we know much more about its benefits and risks, in much larger studies.” Said Simon Baron-Cohen.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: Uta Frith, who studies autism at University College London, said: “According to this study, oxytocin may have the effect of making faces more interesting as assessed by greater activity in brain structures concerned with reward evaluation.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: Disappointingly, this effect is seen only in brain activity and not in behaviour.
label: ignore

title: item_p0023
complex: Demonstrating an effect on behaviour will be critical if nasal spray treatment is to be of any value.”
label: ignore

title: item_p0024
complex: Health warnings covering nearly two-thirds of cigarette packs and a ban on menthol cigarettes across the EU have come a step nearer following a vote in the European Parliament.
label: ignore

title: item_p0024
complex: Menthol and other flavours will be banned from 2022, but, in a blow to the UK government, MEPs decided that most electronic cigarettes, increasingly popular as alternatives to tobacco products, need not be regulated in the same way as medicines.
label: ignore

title: item_p0024
complex: Health officials and the e-cigarette industry in Britain are seeking to clarify what this means – for instance, whether companies in the fast-expanding market face the same bans on sponsorship and promotion at sports events as tobacco firms.
label: ignore

title: item_p0024
complex: The Department of Health would not comment on the advertising issue until officials had studied the MEPs’ decisions.
label: ignore

title: item_p0024
complex: But, in a statement, the DH said: “We are very pleased to see the move towards tougher action on tobacco, with Europe-wide controls banning flavoured cigarettes and the introduction of stricter rules on front-of-pack health warnings.
label: ignore

title: item_p0024
complex: “However, we are disappointed with the decision to reject the proposal to regulate nicotine-containing products (NCPs), including e-cigarettes, as medicines.
label: ignore

title: item_p0024
complex: We believe these products need to be regulated as medicines and will continue to make this point during further negotiations.
label: ignore

title: item_p0024
complex: “Figures show smoking levels in England are at their lowest since records began – 19.5 per cent – but we are determined to further reduce rates of smoking and believe this important step will help.” The UK e-cigarette industry, which broadly welcomed the parliament’s vote, said it was already in talks with the Advertising Standards Authority, but added that it would not be “sensible, proportionate, reasonable or useful” to ban all advertising.
label: ignore

title: item_p0024
complex: MEPs decided e-cigarettes should only be regulated as medical products if manufacturers claimed they could prevent tobacco smoking – a decision criticized by the government’s main medicines regulator.
label: ignore

title: item_p0024
complex: They want to put the products, used by an estimated 1.3 million people in Britain by 2014, on the same legal basis as gums, patches and mouth sprays aimed at helping smokers to quit, but the industry says the expensive process of licensing would help force alternatives to tobacco off the shelves.

title: item_p0024
complex: The MEPs voted to put health warnings on 65% of each cigarette pack, as opposed to a proposed 75%.

title: item_p0024
complex: At present, the warnings cover at least 30% on the front and 40% on the back.

title: item_p0024
complex: The UK government has delayed a decision on whether to follow Australia by introducing standardized packaging until there is evidence that such measures cut tobacco use.

title: item_p0024

title: item_p0024

title: item_p0024
complex: The UK continues to believe that medicinal regulation of NCPs is the best way to deliver a benefit to public health,” said a spokesman.

title: item_p0024
complex: Linda McAvan, Labour MEP for Yorkshire and the Humber and spokesperson on tobacco issues for the parliament’s Socialists & Democrats group, said: “We know that it is children, not adults, who start smoking.
label: ignore

title: item_p0024
complex: And, despite the downward trend in most member states of adult smokers, the World Health Organization figures show worrying upward trends in a number of our member states of young smokers.
label: ignore

title: item_p0024
complex: “We need to stop tobacco companies targeting young people with an array of gimmicky products and we need to make sure that cigarette packs carry effective warnings.” Martin Callanan, the Conservative MEP for North East England, said: “Forcing e-cigs off the shelves would have been totally crazy.
label: ignore

title: item_p0024
complex: These are products that have helped countless people stop smoking more harmful cigarettes and yet some MEPs wanted to make them harder to manufacture than ordinary tobacco.” Katherine Devlin, president of ECITA, the e-cigarette industry association, said “the really important” decision by MEPs not to support medicines regulation meant that was now off the table.

title: item_p0025
complex: Margaret Thatcher, the most dominant British prime minister since Winston Churchill in 1940 and a global champion of the late 20th-century free market economic revival, has died.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: The British government announced that she would receive a ceremonial funeral with military honours at St Paul’s Cathedral.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: The British prime minister, David Cameron, who is cutting short his trip to Europe to return to London following the news, said: “It was with great sadness that l learned of Lady Thatcher’s death.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: We’ve lost a great leader, a great prime minister and a great Briton.” He added: “As our first woman prime minister, Margaret Thatcher succeeded against all the odds, and the real thing about Margaret Thatcher is that she didn’t just lead our country, she saved our country, and I believe she will go down as the greatest British peacetime prime minister.” In a statement, President Barack Obama said, “Here in America, many of us will never forget her standing shoulder to shoulder with President Reagan, reminding the world that we are not simply carried along by the currents of history – we can shape them with moral conviction, unyielding courage and iron will.” The first woman elected to lead a major western state, Lady Thatcher, as she became after the longest premiership since 1827, served 11 unbroken years at No 10 Downing Street.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: Thatcher, who was 87, had been in declining health for some years, suffering from dementia.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: After a series of mini-strokes in 2002, Thatcher withdrew from public life, no longer able to make the kind of waspish pronouncements that had been her forte in office – and beyond.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: Her death was greeted with tributes from across the political spectrum.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: As Labour sources announced the party would suspend campaigning in local elections as a mark of respect, its leader, Ed Miliband, said: “She will be remembered as a unique figure.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: She reshaped the politics of a whole generation.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: She was Britain’s first woman prime minister.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: She moved the centre ground of British politics and was a huge figure on the world stage.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: The Labour Party disagreed with much of what she did and she will always remain a controversial figure.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: But we can disagree and also greatly respect her political achievements and her personal strength.” The deputy prime minister, Nick Clegg, said: “Margaret Thatcher was one of the defining figures in modern British politics.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: Whatever side of the political debate you stand on, no one can deny that as prime minister she left a unique and lasting imprint on the country she served.” Describing her as a political phenomenon, the former Tory prime minister Sir John Major said: “Her outstanding characteristics will always be remembered by those who worked closely with her: courage and determination in politics, and humanity and generosity of spirit in private.” The “Iron Lady” proved a significant cold war ally of the US president Ronald Reagan in the final showdown with the Soviet Union, which broke up under reformist pressures led by Mikhail Gorbachev, a Kremlin leader with whom Thatcher famously declared she could “do business”.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: As a result, many ordinary voters in ex-Soviet bloc states saw her as a bold champion of their liberty, a view widely shared across the spectrum of mainstream US opinion – though not at home or among key EU partners.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: Thatcher was an unremarkable mid-ranking Conservative until she unexpectedly became party leader in 1975.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: Within a decade, she had become known around the world – both admired and detested – for her pro-market domestic reforms and her implacable attitudes in foreign policy, including her long-running battle with the IRA, which almost managed to murder her when it placed a bomb in the Grand Hotel, Brighton, in 1984.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: At home, the emerging doctrine of Thatcherism meant denationalization of state-owned industry – the new word “privatization” came into widespread use in many countries – and defeat of militant trade unionists, notably the National Union of Miners, whose year-long strike (1984– 85) was bitter and traumatic.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: Boosted by the newly arrived revenues from Britain’s North Sea oil fields, Thatcher had room to manoeuvre and change the ageing industrial economy and she used the opportunity to defeat her enemies – including moderate members of her own party.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: But she also deployed her notorious “handbaggings”, or verbal attacks, in the European Union to obtain a British rebate – “my money” as she called it.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: She was less successful in fending off the centralizing ambitions of the “Belgian Empire”, her description of the European Commission, especially in the years when it was headed by the French socialist Jacques Delors.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: Her allies in the tabloid press egged her on.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: And, as the British economy recovered from the severe recession that her monetarist medicine had inflicted on it – to tame the unions and cure inflation – she briefly seemed invincible.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: But untrammelled power, with the defeat or retirement of allies who had kept her in check, led to mistakes and growing unpopularity.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: When Sir Geoffrey Howe, nominally her deputy, finally fell out with Thatcher – chiefly over Europe – his devastating resignation speech triggered a leadership challenge.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: Thatcher made way for John Major rather than risk losing to him in a ballot.
label: ignore

title: item_p0025
complex: In retirement, she wrote highly successful memoirs in two volumes and campaigned energetically on behalf of the Thatcher Foundation, which sought to promote her values around the world.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: Wales will become the first country in the UK where people will be presumed to have consented for their organs to be donated unless they opt out.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: The Welsh Assembly has voted to adopt the opt-out policy, which will allow hospitals to act on the assumption that people who die want to donate unless they have specifically registered an objection.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: The final stage of a bill to adopt a system of presumed consent was passed by 43 votes to eight, with two abstentions, in spite of objections from religious groups on moral grounds and concerns that the scheme could add to the distress of grieving families.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: “This is a huge day for Wales, for devolution and, most importantly, for the 226 people in Wales waiting for an organ transplant,” said the Welsh Health Minister, Mark Drakeford.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: “I am proud that Wales will be the first nation in the UK to take this step.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: As a society, we have shown we are prepared to take action to increase organ donation and to provide hope to those people waiting every week for a transplant.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: “Family refusal is a major factor that affects the numbers of organ donations and the main reason for refusal is lack of knowledge of their loved one’s wishes.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: “The family of the potential donor has a major role to play in organ donation.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: The aim of the bill has always been to respect the wishes of the deceased; however, relatives or friends may object to consent being deemed based on what they know about the views of the deceased.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: “When family members know that organ donation is what the deceased would have wanted, they usually agree to participate in the donation process.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: The new law will work by clarifying people’s wishes around the issue of organ donation and, in turn, increase the rate of consent to donation.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: “Today is a landmark day for Wales and I expect the rest of the UK to be watching with great interest when the legislation is implemented in 2015.” The issue is controversial, with opponents worried that the pressing need for more kidneys and hearts will lead to the wishes of those who have died and their family being overruled.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: But ministers insist there will be safeguards.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: Inevitably, some people will not get around to registering their opposition.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: In response to concerns, the government recently announced that families would play a bigger role.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: Relatives are to have a “clear right of objection”, giving them the chance to show that the deceased would not have wanted to be an organ donor.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: Wales has acted because of an acute shortage of organs.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: “We have the enduring problem of not having enough organs for people who need them,” said Drakeford.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: “About one person every week dies in Wales while on a waiting list.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: We have been working to improve the rate of organ donation and have had some success, but we’re looking to take the next step forward.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: “Around a third of the Welsh population is on the organ donor register, but well over twothirds in surveys say they are happy to be organ donors.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: That other third is people who don’t get round to putting their names down.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: We’re hoping to make inroads into that.” The new law would apply to anybody over 18 who has lived in Wales for at least the year before his or her death.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: Donated organs would not only go to people in need of a transplant in Wales but to anybody in the UK.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: Doctors are delighted at the move.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: The British Medical Association has long campaigned for an opt-out system because of its concern over the growing number of people needing transplants – a result of medical progress in transplantation.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: The number of young donors dropped substantially when seatbelt legislation came in.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: Big efforts have been made in recent years to increase the number of those who carry an organ donation card, with a good deal of success.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: Hospitals have also brought in improved systems for coordinating transplants, including the crucial discussions with relatives when there is no indication of the wishes of the deceased.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: But the increase in numbers of organs harvested is still not enough.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: Some religious groups, on the other hand, strongly oppose the scheme, arguing that it would cause further distress to bereaved relatives.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: Members of the Muslim Council of Wales and the South Wales Jewish Representative Council have expressed reservations, while the Archbishop of Wales, Barry Morgan, said that “donation ought to be a gift of love, of generosity.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: If organs can be taken unless someone has explicitly registered an objection, that’s not an expression of love.
label: ignore

title: item_p0026
complex: It’s more a medical use of a body.”
label: ignore

title: item_p0027
complex: He is not the first person to express scepticism about Mars One, a vastly ambitious private mission aiming to settle humans on Mars from 2025.
label: ignore

title: item_p0027
complex: But Joseph Roche is different from most critics: he’s on the shortlist of astronauts.
label: ignore

title: item_p0027
complex: Roche, an astrophysicist at Trinity College Dublin who was announced in February as among the 100 people in line for the mission, has written for the Guardian expressing his grave doubts about the viability of Mars One.
label: ignore

title: item_p0027
complex: The selection process, Roche writes, “was not rigorous enough to reach the requisite standard of more traditional astronaut selection programmes”.
label: ignore

title: item_p0027
complex: He also says the Dutch Mars One team have displayed “a certain naivety” in believing they can succeed alone in the supposed $6bn mission and should now accept it is very unlikely to happen.
label: ignore

title: item_p0027
complex: He writes: “More openness and transparency would benefit Mars One greatly but I think that the shortcomings of the selection process, coupled with their unwillingness to engage and collaborate with the scientific community mean that the time might have come for Mars One to acknowledge the implausibility of this particular venture and turn their efforts towards supporting other exciting and more viable upcoming space missions.” Roche also expressed worries about the way the mission organizers publicized a so-called top-ten candidates.
label: ignore

title: item_p0027
complex: The ranking, he said, didn’t mean these were the most likely potential astronauts but was, instead, based on how many “supporter points” each had earned through acts such as buying official merchandise.
label: ignore

title: item_p0027
complex: He writes: “These points are Mars One’s supporter points which ‘represent the degree of your support to Mars One’s mission’.” These points play no role in the selection process and serve only to show how much each supporter has donated to Mars One.” The official timeline for the mission says the group plans to dispatch a stationary lander and satellite to Mars in 2018, followed by a rover in 2020 and cargo missions starting in 2022.
label: ignore

title: item_p0027
complex: Humans would start arriving in 2025 and crews of four would be sent every two years to add to the settlement.
label: ignore

title: item_p0027
complex: They would not return to Earth.
label: ignore

title: item_p0027
complex: In February, a prominent supporter of the project, Gerard ’t Hooft, a Dutch Nobel laureate in physics, said he did not believe this timetable was realistic.
label: ignore

title: item_p0027
complex: He said: “It will take quite a bit longer and be quite a bit more expensive.
label: ignore

title: item_p0027
complex: When they first asked me to be involved, I told them: ‘You have to put a zero after everything’.” Roche also spoke to Medium, a US blogging platform that has previously expressed grave sceptism about Mars One, reporting among other things that the supposed 200,000 applications to be astronauts in fact totalled 2,761.
label: ignore

title: item_p0027
complex: He told Medium about the selection process in more detail: “I have not met anyone from Mars One in person.
label: ignore

title: item_p0027
complex: Initially, they’d said there were going to be regional interviews; we would travel there, we’d be interviewed, we’d be tested over several days and, in my mind, that sounded at least like something that approached a legitimate astronaut selection process.
label: ignore

title: item_p0027
complex: “But then they made us sign a non-disclosure agreement if we wanted to be interviewed and then, all of a sudden, it changed from being a proper regional interview over several days to being a ten-minute Skype call.” Roche told the Guardian he did not want to give more interviews as he was wary about being negative about the idea of space travel.
label: ignore

title: item_p0027
complex: In his comment piece he writes: “I am passionate about pushing the boundaries of scientific endeavour and that is why the ambitiousness of the Mars One plan appealed to me.
label: ignore

title: item_p0027
complex: Although Mars One were never likely to overcome the financial and technical barriers during their proposed timeline, it was refreshing to hear a new idea that challenges us to think about our own role in the future of space exploration.
label: ignore

title: item_p0027
complex: “Being part of the subsequent public debate over the ethics and morality of future missions has been one of the most interesting and enjoyable aspects of my candidacy with Mars One.
label: ignore

title: item_p0027
complex: If a one-way mission to Mars ever became possible, then I would always volunteer.
label: ignore

title: item_p0027
complex: For an astrophysicist, that is not a difficult decision to make but it is also a moot point because I do not think we will see a one-way mission in my lifetime.”
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: Serial dater Emmanuel Limal was tired of meeting women who weren’t ready to start a family, or at least wouldn’t admit that they were.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: The 43-year-old actor, originally from France, had spent 20 years living in Copenhagen and looking for love in the hope of raising children.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: He recently took his quest online but was dismayed by the results.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: “I got frustrated with everyone trying to sell themselves as really active, always travelling or with a long list of hobbies, but no mention of children,” Limal said.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: “On some sites, there was an option to click, saying: ‘I’d like kids someday,’ but you would read the person’s profile and think: ‘You will never have time!’ If someone’s going to the gym eight times a week and travelling every month, they are not putting a family first.” Limal has a six-year-old daughter from a previous relationship, but coming from a big family – his father is one of 11 – he has always wanted more children.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: “I couldn’t seem to meet anyone willing to prioritize starting a family and struggled with when to mention wanting kids any time I met someone new.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: It’s the ultimate dating taboo,” he said.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: “Then one day I read a profile from a 38-year-old who said she knew it was ‘really bad to admit’ but she wanted children.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: And I just thought: ‘You shouldn’t be ashamed of this.’” Limal remortgaged his apartment to fund the setting up of Babyklar.nu – or ‘baby-ready now’ in English.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: It functions like a normal dating site but every potential dater is asked to be honest about their wish to start a family soon.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: “We ask people if they are OK with someone who already has children as well as wanting another baby,” Limal said.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: “But we don’t make them specify how many children they’d like.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: That would be a bit too much like grocery shopping online.” The response to the site has been overwhelming, he said.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: “We had 50 sign-ups an hour when we launched in June and we are already hearing from couples who have met through the site and are now together.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: I’m fully expecting the first Babyklar.nu baby by next summer.” More men have signed up than women (53% to 47%), with testimonials such as “It’s so lovely to be able to say this out loud” and “I finally dare to be honest about what I want.” The site has come at an opportune time for the country of five million people.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: Danes are not having enough babies, according to a report from the Copenhagen hospital Rigshospitalet, and the current rate of 1.7 children per family is not enough to maintain Denmark’s population.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: The usual suspects are being blamed for the new low – women leaving it “too late” and couples cohabiting and waiting to start families.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: “Now, I hope, men and women who want to start a family but haven’t met the right person yet will have another option,” says Limal.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: He’s keen to point out that this isn’t just about baby farming: “I want this to be about children and love.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: My goal is to pair up people who really want a family and a partner – and who’ll stay together.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: I’m a romantic at heart.” There are plans to roll out the site in France and the UK later in 2013, but for now it is the Danes who are reaping the benefits.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: “Danes have no problem having children before marriage so things can move fast and, because the country’s so small, a Jutlander can date a Copenhagener without too much travel,” Limal said.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: What’s more, Limal has finally found love.
label: ignore

title: item_p0028
complex: “I’ve met a nice woman and she wants a baby too – so we shall see.”
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: The National Security Agency (NSA) has obtained direct access to the systems of Google, Facebook, Apple and other US internet giants, according to a top-secret document.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: The NSA access is part of a previously undisclosed program called PRISM, which allows officials to collect material including search history, the content of emails, file transfers and live chats, the document says.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: The Guardian has verified the authenticity of the document, a 41-slide PowerPoint presentation – classified as top secret with no distribution to foreign allies – which was apparently used to train intelligence operatives on the capabilities of the program.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: The document claims “collection directly from the servers” of major US service providers.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: Although the presentation claims the program is run with the assistance of the companies, all those who responded to a request for comment denied knowledge of any such program.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: In a statement, Google said: “Google cares deeply about the security of our users’ data.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: We disclose user data to government in accordance with the law and we review all such requests carefully.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: From time to time, people allege that we have created a government ‘back door’ into our systems, but Google does not have a back door for the government to access private user data.” Several senior tech executives insisted that they had no knowledge of PRISM or of any similar scheme.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: They said they would never have been involved in such a program.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: “If they are doing this, they are doing it without our knowledge,” one said.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: An Apple spokesman said he had “never heard” of PRISM.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: The NSA access was enabled by changes to US surveillance law, introduced under President Bush and renewed under Obama in December 2012.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: The program facilitates extensive, in-depth surveillance on live communications and stored information.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: The law allows for the targeting of any customers of participating firms who live outside the US, or those Americans whose communications include people outside the US.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: It also opens the possibility of communications made entirely within the US being collected without warrants.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: Disclosure of the PRISM program follows a leak to the Guardian on Wednesday of a top-secret court order compelling telecoms provider Verizon to turn over the telephone records of millions of US customers.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: The participation of the internet companies in PRISM will add to the debate about the scale of surveillance by the intelligence services.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: Unlike the collection of those call records, this surveillance can include the content of communications and not just the metadata Some of the world’s largest internet brands are claimed to be part of the information-sharing program since its introduction in 2007.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: Microsoft – which is currently running an advertising campaign with the slogan “Your privacy is our priority” – was the first, with collection beginning in December 2007.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: It was followed by Yahoo in 2008; Google, Facebook and PalTalk in 2009; YouTube in 2010; Skype and AOL in 2011; and finally Apple, which joined the program in 2012.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: The program is continuing to expand, with other providers due to come online.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: Collectively, the companies cover the vast majority of online email, search, video and communications networks.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: The extent and nature of the data collected from each company varies.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: Companies are legally obliged to comply with requests for users’ communications under US law, but the PRISM program allows the intelligence services direct access to the companies’ servers.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: The NSA document notes the operations have the “assistance of communications providers in the US”.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: The revelation also supports concerns raised by several US senators during the renewal of the FISA Amendments Act (FAA) in December 2012, who warned about the scale of surveillance the law might enable and shortcomings in the safeguards it introduces.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: When the FAA was first enacted, defenders of the statute argued that a significant check on abuse would be the NSA’s inability to obtain electronic communications without the consent of the telecom and internet companies that control the data.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: But the PRISM program renders that consent unnecessary, as it allows the agency to directly and unilaterally seize the communications off the companies’ servers.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: A chart prepared by the NSA, contained within the top-secret document, highlights the breadth of the data it is able to obtain: email, video and voice chat, videos, photos, voice-over-IP (Skype, for example) chats, file transfers, social networking details and more.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: The document is recent, dating to April 2013.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: Such a leak is extremely rare in the history of the NSA, which prides itself on maintaining a high level of secrecy.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: The PRISM program allows the NSA, the world’s largest surveillance organization, to obtain targeted communications without having to request them from the service providers and without having to obtain individual court orders.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: With this program, the NSA is able to reach directly into the servers of the participating companies and obtain both stored communications and perform real-time collection on targeted users.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: A senior administration official said in a statement: “The Guardian and Washington Post articles refer to collection of communications pursuant to Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA).
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: This law does not allow the targeting of any US citizen or of any person located within the United States.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: The program is subject to oversight by the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Court, the Executive Branch and Congress.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: It involves extensive procedures, specifically approved by the court, to ensure that only non-US persons outside the US are targeted and that minimize the acquisition, retention and dissemination of incidentally acquired information about US persons.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: “This program was recently reauthorized by Congress after extensive hearings and debate.
label: ignore

title: item_p0029
complex: Information collected under this program is among the most important and valuable intelligence information we collect and is used to protect our nation from a wide variety of threats.”
label: ignore

title: item_p0030

complex: They included the notorious former Brazilian federation chief Ricardo Teixeira and his successor, Marco Polo Del Nero, who has recently stepped down from the FIFA executive committee.
