title: item_p0073
complex: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has appealed to Washington to sort out its finances after the US pulled back from the brink of a debt default and hundreds of thousands of federal employees returned to work after a 16-day government shutdown.
label: ignore

title: item_p0073
complex: As the US President, Barack Obama, warned “We’ve got to get out of the habit of governing by crisis,” the IMF’s managing director, Christine Lagarde, appealed for more stability.
label: ignore

title: item_p0073
complex: “It will be essential to reduce uncertainty surrounding the conduct of fiscal policy by raising the debt limit in a more durable manner,” she said.
label: ignore

title: item_p0073
complex: “We also continue to encourage the US to approve a budget for 2014 and replace the sequester with gradually phased-in measures that would not harm the recovery, and to adopt a balanced and comprehensive medium-term fiscal plan.” A Senate-drafted peace deal that contained almost no concessions to the conservatives who had driven the country to the precipice of a new financial crisis was passed by the Republican-dominated House of Representatives just hours before a deadline to extend the US debt limit was to pass.
label: ignore

title: item_p0073
complex: The World Bank, too, expressed its relief that the global economy had “dodged a potential catastrophe”, with its president, Jim Yong Kim, urging policymakers in all countries to “continue to focus on crafting and implementing policies that promote economic growth and boost jobs and opportunity for all”.
label: ignore

title: item_p0073
complex: Stock markets in Japan, China, Hong Kong and South Korea initially reflected relief after the Republicans finally capitulated in their failed attempt to undermine Obama’s healthcare reforms.
label: ignore

title: item_p0073
complex: But, in Asia and Europe, stock markets overall displayed a muted reaction, with traders apparently expecting another battle in Washington early in 2014.
label: ignore

title: item_p0073
complex: The shutdown is estimated to have cost the US $24bn, according to the ratings agency Standard & Poor’s.
label: ignore

title: item_p0073
complex: China’s official Xinhua News Agency had accused Washington of jeopardizing other countries’ dollar assets.
label: ignore

title: item_p0073
complex: China is the US government’s largest creditor.
label: ignore

title: item_p0073
complex: Obama signed the necessary legislation to fend off a default shortly after midnight on Thursday after a Republican split in the House of Representatives.
label: ignore

title: item_p0073
complex: The bill had passed easily with broad bipartisan support in the Senate, where Democratic and Republican leaders forged the agreement.
label: ignore

title: item_p0073
complex: It offers a temporary fix, funding the government until 15 January and raising the debt ceiling until 7 February.
label: ignore

title: item_p0073
complex: But the president made clear he did not expect another bitter budget fight and shutdown in 2014.
label: ignore

title: item_p0073
complex: In brief remarks at the White House shortly before the House vote, Obama said he hoped the deal would “lift the cloud of uncertainty” that had hung over the country in recent weeks.
label: ignore

title: item_p0073
complex: “Once this agreement arrives on my desk, I will sign it immediately,” he said, in a statement delivered at the White House.
label: ignore

title: item_p0073
complex: “Hopefully, next time it won’t be in the eleventh hour.
label: ignore

title: item_p0073
complex: We’ve got to get out of the habit of governing by crisis.” As he left the lectern after his Wednesday night press briefing, the president was asked by a journalist whether the crisis would happen all over again in a few months.
label: ignore

title: item_p0073
complex: Speaking over his shoulder, Obama replied, “No.” Earlier, the Republican senator Mike Lee had struck a defiant tone, perhaps indicating more trouble ahead: “The media keeps asking: ‘Was it worth it?’ My answer is, it is always worth it to do the right thing.” He added: “This is not over.” However, the political deal was one of the worst of all possible outcomes for Republicans.
label: ignore

title: item_p0073
complex: None of their stated goals was achieved and polls showed that voters overwhelmingly blamed them for the crisis.
label: ignore

title: item_p0074
complex: Glastonbury Festival is to combat the scourge of the plastic water bottle as part of a longterm strategy to become the world’s most environmentally friendly outdoor music event.
label: ignore

title: item_p0074
complex: Festival organizers are targeting the disposable bottle, one of the most conspicuous symbols of the throwaway culture, that each year leaves the 900-acre Somerset site wreathed in plastic, with an estimated one million plastic bottles being used during the festival.
label: ignore

title: item_p0074
complex: Stainless-steel reusable bottles will be given to 2,000 road crew and band members, with thousands more on sale to festival-goers, to stop them relying on plastic bottles.
label: ignore

title: item_p0074
complex: The 140,000 ticket-holders are also being urged to bring reusable bottles that they can fill at 400 drinkingwater taps dotted across the site.
label: ignore

title: item_p0074
complex: Lucy Smith, Glastonbury’s green issues organizer, said: “We have amazing water quality in the UK but everyone is obsessed with drinking bottled water.” She said the initiative precedes a plan for Glastonbury 2015 to replace all plastic beer glasses and cutlery with reusable items in an attempt to eradicate the legacy of plastic waste from the huge rural site.
label: ignore

title: item_p0074
complex: Environmentalists estimate that 150 million tonnes of plastic waste currently litters the planet and oceans, poisoning ecosystems and killing wildlife.
label: ignore

title: item_p0074
complex: Ultimately, festival organizers hope to make Glastonbury the world’s greenest greenfield festival, emulating America’s Burning Man festival in the Black Rock Desert of Nevada, which is a “leave-no-trace” event, where people have to take away all that they bring.
label: ignore

title: item_p0074
complex: Glastonbury revellers are also being urged to travel to the site on public transport or try car-sharing with friends.
label: ignore

title: item_p0074
complex: “We want to be as sustainable as we can.
label: ignore

title: item_p0074
complex: We do everything we can, but coping with the litter of 140,000 people is a challenge.
label: ignore

title: item_p0074
complex: We can’t put bins everywhere,” added Smith Campaigners say that plastic water bottles can take hundreds or even thousands of years to completely biodegrade, with their manufacture exacerbating their negative ecological impact.
label: ignore

title: item_p0074
complex: Millions of barrels of oil are used in the manufacture of plastic bottles and the transportation of mineral water across the planet produces even more carbon emissions.
label: ignore

title: item_p0074
complex: Overall, an estimated 13 billion plastic water bottles are sold in the UK every year, yet just one in five is said to be recycled.
label: ignore

title: item_p0074
complex: Smith said that, instead of buying bottled water, festival-goers should take advantage of the water on tap, which is being drawn from huge underground reservoirs, instead of old-fashioned water tanks that provided heavily chlorinated drinking water.
label: ignore

title: item_p0074
complex: The charity WaterAid will also set up water kiosks around the site, stocking reusable bottles and cups, and offering free refills.
label: ignore

title: item_p0074
complex: In 2015, the kiosks – modelled on those found in Africa – will double as DJ booths at night.
label: ignore

title: item_p0074
complex: Organizers say that almost half of all the rubbish left on site was recycled in 2013 and add that there will be 15,000 bins for recycling across the festival grounds in 2014.
label: ignore

title: item_p0074
complex: Despite its growing eco-credentials, critics continue to snipe at Glastonbury, accusing it of becoming increasingly corporate in tone.
label: ignore

title: item_p0074
complex: The latest critic, Iron Maiden’s Bruce Dickinson, has vowed never to bring his band to Glastonbury Festival after dismissing it as “the most bourgeois thing on the planet”.
label: ignore

title: item_p0074
complex: The weather forecast for Glastonbury was positive, with the festival due to be sunny and dry, experts ruling out a repeat of 1985, the festival’s windiest year; 1997, its muddiest; and 2005, known as the “year of thunder”.
label: ignore

title: item_p0075
complex: When it comes to climate change, we have the bad habit of focusing on the rst part of the story, the part about the problem, and forgetting the second part about the many available solutions.
label: ignore

title: item_p0075
complex: These solutions are speeding up recycling, slowing down emissions and providing sustainable alternatives to plastic, air conditioning, smartphones and fast fashion.
label: ignore

title: item_p0075
complex: The Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change recently gathered in Copenhagen to present its latest report on the impacts and pace of climate change.
label: ignore

title: item_p0075
complex: Climate change is now measured on all continents and our efforts to lower emissions must be intensi ed to avoid it escalating out of control.
label: ignore

title: item_p0075
complex: Along with outlining the risks and challenges, Copenhagen also embraced and focused on the solutions.
label: ignore

title: item_p0075
complex: In the spirit of focusing on what can be done, Sustainia Award, chaired by Arnold Schwarzenegger, celebrated ten leading sustainability solutions deployed in 84 countries.
label: ignore

title: item_p0075
complex: From food to fashion, energy to transportation, education to health, the awards showcased an alternative to the grim-future scenarios we are so often presented with and made sustainability tangible to the innovators, investors, consumers and policy makers across sectors and regions.
label: ignore

title: item_p0075
complex: From California, we saw how we can now produce plastics from greenhouse gases that are competitive with normal oil-based plastics in price and quality.
label: ignore

title: item_p0075
complex: From Switzerland, we learned how we can recycle and reuse old clothes and shoes more effectively in a recycle system currently deployed in over 60 countries.
label: ignore

title: item_p0075
complex: And from Canada, we learned how smartphones can make bike- sharing more convenient.
label: ignore

title: item_p0075
complex: The ten projects presented each offered unique solutions to sustainability challenges but it was the Nigerian initiative, Wecyclers, that won Arnold SchwarzeneggerÕs, and the rest of juryÕs, vote and took the Sustainia Award 2014.
label: ignore

title: item_p0075
complex: Wecyclers enables low-income communities to make money on waste piling up in their streets.
label: ignore

title: item_p0075
complex: By deploying a eet of cargo bicycles to collect and recycle unmanaged waste in Lagos, Wecyclers lets families exchange garbage for consumer goods via an SMS-based point system.
label: ignore

title: item_p0075
complex: Recycling companies purchase WecyclersÕ sorted waste for reprocessing into products Ð they turn it into mattresses, pillows and trash bags.
label: ignore

title: item_p0075
complex: Wecyclers is a response to local waste issues, where itÕs estimated that only 40% of the cityÕs rubbish is collected.
label: ignore

title: item_p0075
complex: According to the World Bank, only 46% of municipal solid waste in Africa is collected.
label: ignore

title: item_p0075
complex: More than 5,000 households have signed up so far and there are plans to extend the initiative to other cities throughout Nigeria.
label: ignore

title: item_p0075
complex: Solutions to combat climate change are often perceived as hi-tech innovations focused on cutting emissions, creating infrastructure or ef ciency.
label: ignore

title: item_p0075
complex: However, to successfully solve the variety of challenges, we need variety in our solutions as well.
label: ignore

title: item_p0075
complex: Sustainability is not solely a matter of bringing down emissions; it is also a question of using our natural resources more intelligently and creating healthier lives for ourselves.
label: ignore

title: item_p0075
complex: Initiatives might be low-tech in innovation but high-impact when it comes to creating sustainable change for entire communities.
label: ignore

title: item_p0075
complex: With a wide range of solutions addressing the equally wide range of challenges, we must focus more on the important part of the story that creates enthusiasm and momentum, and spurs action for much-needed change.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: Vienna is the worldÕs best city to live in, Baghdad is the worst and London, Paris and New York do not even make it into the top 35, according to international research into quality of life.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: German-speaking cities dominate the rankings in the 18th Mercer Quality of Life study, with Vienna joined by Zurich, Munich, Dusseldorf and Frankfurt in the top seven.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: Paris has tumbled down the league, falling ten places to 37th, just ahead of London at 39th, almost entirely because of the cityÕs vulnerability to terrorist attacks.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: The study examined social and economic conditions, health, education, housing and the environment, and is used by big companies to assess where they should locate and how much they should pay staff.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: Viennese-born Helena Hartlauer, 32, said she was not surprised at her cityÕs top position.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: The municipalityÕs social democratic government has a long tradition of investing in high-quality social housing, making Vienna almost uniquely affordable among major cities.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: ÒI live in a 100sq-metre turn-of-the-century apartment in a good area about 20 minutesÕ walk from the city centre.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: But my rent is just Û800 (£625) a month.Ó An equivalent apartment in London would cost upwards of £2,000 and even more in New York, ranked 44th in the table.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: US cities perform relatively poorly in the study, largely because of issues around personal safety and crime.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: The highest ranking city in the US is San Francisco, at 28th; Boston is 34th.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: Canadian cities, led by Vancouver, far outrank their US rivals in the table.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: ÒYou donÕt realize how safe Vienna is until you head abroad,Ó said Hartlauer.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: ÒWe also have terri c public transport, with the underground working 24 hours at weekends, and it only costs Û1 per trip.Ó Vienna bene ted enormously from the fall of the Berlin Wall, becoming the gateway to Eastern European countries that often have historic ties to the former Austro-Hungarian empire.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: ÒOur big USP is our geographical location,Ó said Martin Eichtinger, Austrian ambassdaor to London, who lived in Vienna for 20 years.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: ÒThe fall of the Berlin Wall helped de ne Vienna as the hub for companies wanting to do business in Central Europe.Ó According to the World Bank, Austria has one of the highest gures for GDP per head in the world, just behind the US and ahead of Germany and Britain, although quite some way below neighbouring Switzerland.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: Zurich in Switzerland is named by Mercer as having the worldÕs second highest quality of life but the Viennese say their city is far more fun.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: ÒThere are more students in Vienna than any other German-speaking city,Ó said Hartlauer.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: ÒItÕs a very fast growing, young and lively city,Ó she added Ð though she conceded she works for the cityÕs tourist board.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: Vienna has long been overlooked by British weekend city break tourists, who instead ock to Barcelona or Berlin and tend to think of Austria as somewhere for skiing, lakes and mountains.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: But, after an increase in budget ights from regional British cities such as Manchester and Edinburgh, Vienna is fast catching up as a popular destination.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: In 2015, there were 588,000 British visitors to Vienna, up 18% on the year before.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: The ow is both ways; Eichtinger said London has become the number one city destination for Austrian visitors.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: ÒVienna has ranked top in the last seven published rankings,Ó said Mercer.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: ÒIt scores highly in a number of categories; it provides a safe and stable environment to live in, a high level of public utilities and transport facilities and good recreational facilities.Ó The European migrant crisis, which has seen large numbers of refugees and asylum seekers pass through Vienna en route to Germany, has had little impact on the city of nearly 1.8 million people, said Eichtinger.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: ÒWe have managed to accommodate 90,000 refugees in Austria but the numbers have slowed in recent months.Ó London has never been in the quality-of-life top ten, says Mercer, damaged by its poor scores for air pollution, traf c congestion and climate.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: After London, Edinburgh is the next-ranking British city, in 46th place.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: Paris has suffered the biggest fall in the most recent rankings.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: ÒParis remained stable for several years but has, this year, dropped ten places in the overall ranking,Ó said Mercer.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: ÒThe drop was essentially due to the terrorist attacks in 2015.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: However, it is important to highlight that safety issues are a very highly weighted factor within the ÔbasketÕ so any small adjustments can have a big impact on the ranking.Ó Auckland in New Zealand was the highest ranking English-speaking city in the survey, in third place, followed by Vancouver in fth.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: Australian cities also perform very highly in the survey, with Sydney 10th and Melbourne 15th.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: The Economist has consistently ranked Melbourne as the worldÕs most liveable city, although its survey has been criticized as too Anglocentric.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: War and political unrest are behind all the worst-ranked cities in the world.
label: ignore

title: item_p0076
complex: Surprisingly, Damascus is named as only the seventh worst, ranked better than not just Baghdad but also Bangui in Central African Republic, SanaÕa in Yemen, Port-au-Prince in Haiti, Khartoum in Sudan and NÕDjamena in Chad.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: A thick crust of bird droppings is piled on the gilded balustrade of one of Britain’s most expensive properties.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: Pigeon skeletons lie among shattered mirrors and water streams through broken cornicing.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: This is The Towers, a £30m palace in “Billionaires’ Row” in north London, whose spectacular ruin has been kept secret until now.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: It is one of ten mansions in the middle of The Bishops Avenue – the heart of London’s spiralling property market – that have stood almost entirely vacant since they were bought a quarter of a century ago, it is believed on behalf of members of the Saudi Arabian royal family.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: Their Grecian columns are cracking into pieces and mosaic-tiled swimming pools are filled with rubble.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: Nature has taken over to the extent that owls have moved in.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: It is a desolate scene repeated up and down the supposedly prestigious avenue that Lloyds Bank has calculated is the second most expensive street in Britain.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: While more and more people struggle to get on to London’s property ladder as house prices rise at 11.2% a year, 16 mansions on the most expensive stretch of The Bishops Avenue are sitting empty, many behind padlocked gates, with their windows shuttered with steel grilles and overgrown grounds patrolled by guard dogs.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: Across the street stands another derelict mansion, worth £18m, with smashed windows and walls coated in anti-climb paint.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: Metal grilles block the windows of another, which has been sold for £20m.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: But that doesn’t stop the prices going up.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: Dryades, a mansion until recently owned by a Pakistani politician, sold for £12m in 2007 and is believed to be worth about £30m today.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: Heath Lodge, the scene of the 1984 murder, by silver bullet, of fashion tycoon Aristos Constantinou, is worth £13m today, after having been sold in the late 1970s for £400,000.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: The dereliction can be agonizing for people struggling to keep a roof above their heads in one of the world’s most expensive cities.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: One security guard working on the avenue said it was exasperating to see so many tens of thousands of square feet of property – enough to house dozens of people – falling apart.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: Royals flushed with oil wealth from Nigeria and Saudi Arabia were among the first to come to this curving road near Hampstead Heath.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: Iranians fled here after the fall of the shah.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: Now, Chinese house hunters are following Russians and Kazakhs who have spent millions securing an address estate agents tell them is as world famous as the Champs Elysées and Rodeo Drive.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: Recently, two mansions have been on sale for £65m and £38m, promising endless Italian marble, leather-padded lifts and luxury panic rooms.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: However, in the grounds of the empty mansions, stone fountains crumble and lawns have become bogs.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: Inside one, water drips through a huge crystal chandelier onto a thick carpet rotting under sections of collapsed ceiling.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: Moss grows through shattered bricks and mirrored tiles are scattered across a bathroom.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: The swimming pool is filled with a foot of brackish water and has flowers growing through its tiles.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: Wooden slats bulge away from the sauna.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: But it is the wreck of The Towers, a grand mansion set among acres of hornbeams, oaks and limes, that is most dramatic, with its huge, high-ceiling halls occupied by pigeons and its walls turned bright green by algae as water pours through three storeys and plinks into a vast, empty, basement swimming pool.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: Unopened wooden crates marked “bullet-proof glass” reveal the security fears of the previous owners.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: Today, very few people live on The Bishops Avenue full time.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: A security guard patrolling the pavement outside one mansion said that the owners were not there.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: Another, outside Royal Mansion, declined to say if anyone was home, while a member of staff at another mansion simply warned the Guardian about the guard dogs.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: Magdy Adib Ishak-Hannah, an Egyptian-born private healthcare mogul, whose personal wealth is £45m, said he was in the minority of permanent residents.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: “It’s not a neighbourly place, where you can chat over the fence,” he said.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: “To be honest, I have never seen what my neighbours look like.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: Next door, a Saudi princess spent £35m on a new house and I’ve never seen her.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: There are about three houses that are lived in 24/7 and half of the properties are occupied three to six months a year.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: The other half, who knows if they come or not?” The multimillion-pound wrecks are evidence of a property culture in which the world’s richest people see British property as investments.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: One Hyde Park, a block of apartments in Knightsbridge, is another example where more than half the flats are registered with the council as empty or second homes.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: Nevertheless, the talk on the avenue is about building £5m apartments, instead of £50m mansions, in an effort to draw people back.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: Anil Varma, a local property developer, has decided to rebuild one of the most valuable sites on the avenue as a collection of 20 apartments with a concierge, maid service, 25-metre pool, spa and cinema.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: “If you build a big house and try and sell for £30m to £40m, it won’t sell,” he said.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: “Locals won’t buy and so you have to bring in overseas buyers.” But the prospect of the avenue’s empty property being used to help solve the housing crisis remains distant.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: Andrew Harper, a local Conservative councillor, laughed when asked whether some of the derelict housing could become affordable homes.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: He said the land price would be prohibitive.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: “Very wealthy people own property there,” he said.
label: ignore

title: item_p0077
complex: “Sometimes they live in them and sometimes they don’t.”
label: ignore

title: item_p0078
complex: They call him the Robin Hood of the banks, a man who took out dozens of loans, worth almost half a million euros, with no intention of ever paying them back.
label: ignore

title: item_p0078
complex: Instead, Enric Duran handed the money out to projects that created and promoted alternatives to capitalism.
label: ignore

title: item_p0078
complex: After 14 months in hiding, Duran is unapologetic, even though his activities could land him in jail.
label: ignore

title: item_p0078
complex: ÒIÕm proud of what I did,Ó he said in an interview by Skype from an undisclosed location.
label: ignore

title: item_p0078
complex: The money, he said, had created opportunities.
label: ignore

title: item_p0078
complex: ÒIt generated a movement that allowed us to push forward with the construction of alternatives.
label: ignore

title: item_p0078
complex: And it allowed us to build a powerful network that groups together these initiatives.Ó From 2006 to 2008, Duran took out 68 commercial and personal loans from 39 banks in Spain.
label: ignore

title: item_p0078
complex: He farmed the money out to social activists, funding speaking tours against capitalism and TV cameras for a media network.
label: ignore

title: item_p0078
complex: ÒI saw that, on one side, these social movements were building alternatives but that they lacked resources and communication capacities,Ó he said.
label: ignore

title: item_p0078
complex: ÒMeanwhile, our reliance on perpetual growth was creating a system that created money out of nothing.Ó The loans he swindled from banks were his way of regulating and denouncing this situation, he said.
label: ignore

title: item_p0078
complex: He started slowly.
label: ignore

title: item_p0078
complex: ÒI lled out a few credit applications with my real details.
label: ignore

title: item_p0078
complex: They denied me, but I just wanted to get a feel for what they were asking for.Ó From there, the former table-tennis coach began to weave an intricate web of accounts, payments and transfers.
label: ignore

title: item_p0078
complex: ÒI was learning constantly.Ó By the summer of 2007, he had discovered how to make the system work, applying for loans under the name of a false television production company.
label: ignore

title: item_p0078
complex: ÒThen, I managed to get a lot.Ó Û492,000, to be exact.
label: ignore

title: item_p0078
complex: Duran was arrested in Spain in 2009, on charges brought against him by six of the 39 banks that had lent him money.
label: ignore

title: item_p0078
complex: He spent two months in prison before being bailed for Û50,000.
label: ignore

title: item_p0078
complex: In February 2013, facing up to eight years in prison, he decided to ee rather than stand trial.
label: ignore

title: item_p0078
complex: ÒI donÕt see legitimacy in a judicial system based on authority, because I donÕt recognize its authority,Ó he said.
label: ignore

title: item_p0078
complex: His actions, he said, were in the vanguard of a worldwide debate on the economic crisis.
label: ignore

title: item_p0078
complex: The timing pushed the anti-capitalist movement into the light, just as many Spaniards were seeking alternatives to a system that had wreaked havoc on their lives.
label: ignore

title: item_p0078
complex: While the same actions would probably be better understood in todayÕs Spain, he said that they would not be needed.

title: item_p0078

title: item_p0079

title: item_p0079
complex: The film, released in February, took more than $250m in the US and £31m in the UK by the first weekend in April.

title: item_p0079
complex: The movie cost about $60m to make and has been described as a near-flawless piece of content marketing by creating entertaining content aimed at consumers who are likely to go out and buy the company’s products.

title: item_p0079
complex: Lego’s finance director, John Goodwin, said: “The strong performance of the Lego Movie products had a positive effect during the first half of 2014, and it remains to be seen how the line will continue to develop behind the highly anticipated launch of the movie on DVD in the second half of 2014.” Operating profit for the first six months of Lego’s financial year increased by 12% to $630m.

title: item_p0080
complex: Dame Sally Davies, the Chief Medical Officer for England, has likened the problem of antibiotic resistance to the risks presented by international terrorism.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: While this might sound like an exaggeration, the threat was actually, if anything, understated.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: Each year, the global number of deaths to which bacterial resistance contributes far outstrips those caused by terrorist attacks.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: While it is difficult to track the global impact of antibiotic resistance across all bacterial species, the World Health Organization estimates that for tuberculosis alone multi-drug resistance accounts for more than 150,000 deaths each year.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: Antibiotic resistance is no longer an abstract risk: this is now a war.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: In the past hundred years, our expectations of life and survival have changed beyond all recognition.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: At the beginning of the twentieth century, life expectancy in the UK stood at around 47 years of age for a man and 50 for a woman, a number heavily affected by the very high rate of infant mortality in those days.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: Around a third of all deaths occurred in children under the age of five, largely because of infectious disease.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: In contrast, a child born in Britain today has a better than one in four chance of reaching their 100th birthday.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: For this we have public health systems, vaccination and antibiotics to thank.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: It is by these means – the prevention and treatment of illnesses caused by microorganisms – that the real war against disease is principally won.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: Elsewhere, we have pushed the limits of survival, notably in intensive care.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: This, the specialism in which I chose to train, is where antibiotic resistant organisms are most prevalent.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: Here, powerful antibiotics, essential in the treatment of life-threatening illness, are used routinely.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: These drugs decimate ordinary bacteria.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: But what they leave behind are hardy species that have begun to learn tricks that allow them to evade antibiotic drugs.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: As a newly qualified doctor in the late 1990s, I learnt about Methicillin Resistant Staphylococcus Aureus – the infamous MRSA – a bacterial species resistant to methicillin and all other penicillins.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: In the fight against it, there were a handful of exotic-sounding drugs – vancomycin and teicoplanin among them.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: These were supposed to be our last lines of defence, but antibiotic resistant bacteria became more and more common; as did species with new patterns of resistance.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: Drugs we had previously barely heard of became commonplace.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: New last-line drugs emerged to replace the old.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: We got used to this state of affairs; a steady escalation in the arms race between us and the bacteria.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: But the balance has been shifting steadily.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: In our hospitals and our GP surgeries we have abused the drugs that gave us such a huge advantage over infectious disease, using them too often and too indiscriminately.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: And some of the worst abuses have occurred outside of healthcare, with antibiotics introduced into the food chain, through agriculture and the lacing of livestock feed with anti-bacterial drugs.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: We assumed that antibiotic therapy was an advantage we could enjoy forever.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: We became complacent that the pharmaceutical industry would continue to stay ahead of the game.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: But this is no longer the case.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: New, more resistant species have been identified.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: The vancomycin that we used to rely on to treat MRSA infection was defeated.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: Vancomycin Resistant Staphylococcus Aureus (VRSA) emerged in our hospitals.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: And other bacterial species were learning the same trick.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: Enterobacteria, organisms usually found in the gut, had also acquired vancomycin resistance.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: Today, infections with formidable, highly resistant organisms are commonplace and the pharmaceutical industry is not keeping pace.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: Fewer and fewer new anti-microbial drugs are emerging from their production lines.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: It is becoming increasingly difficult to develop new drugs active against resistant strains.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: For every method of attack the pharmaceutical companies invent, bacteria rapidly evolve a defence.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: All of the simple approaches to the problem have been exhausted.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: Antibiotics have become drugs that are expensive to develop, that are only used in short courses and that quickly become ineffective due to the evolution of bacterial resistance.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: Consequently, the pharmaceutical industry’s incentive to manufacture new drugs that can fight them is low.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: Almost as soon as antibiotic use became widespread in the 1940s, the first evidence of bacteria resistance to antimicrobial therapy emerged.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: Initially, these were little more than curiosities.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: When they did infect patients, the numbers were so small that at first they were not enough to warrant much attention.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: But today, they have become a fact of medical life.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: Less than a century after the discovery of penicillin, we are beginning to lose the fight.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: Since the first MRSA deaths in otherwise healthy children in the US in 1998, the number of deaths from MRSA infection in the US each year has risen to tens of thousands – outstripping the number of deaths caused by AIDS.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: Bacterial resistance in hospitals is everywhere you look.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: This is a war like no other.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: There needs to be cultural change in our prescribing behaviours and more restraint in the use of antibiotics in farming and agriculture.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: And somehow, the pharmaceutical companies have to be convinced to chase the development of these less profitable drugs.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: Within my working lifetime, the pattern of antibiotic resistance in healthcare has transformed from a rare but notable event to a problem of epidemic proportion.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: If we are to avoid a return to the pre-antibiotic time with all its excess mortality, we must be bold.
label: ignore

title: item_p0080
complex: To squander the advantage we have so recently gained against microorganisms in the fight for life would be unthinkable.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: There are eyes on you, behind the bright lights and mirrored panels.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: Pick up a boot and a camera will make sure you donÕt slip it into your bag.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: Enter a department store and you will be watched.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: But new technology is leading retailers to grow a different set of eyes Ð less focused on shoplifting and more interested in your age, sex, size, head, shoulders, knees and toes.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: A few months ago, IT rm Computer Sciences Corporation (CSC) produced a report that claimed around 30% of retailers use facial recognition technology to track customers in-store.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: Facial recognition is a technology that can identify people by analysing and comparing facial features from a database, using devices such as Intel RealSense cameras, which are able to analyse everything from particular expressions to the clothing brands someone is wearing.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: Joe Jensen, of IntelÕs Retail Solutions Division, says that the aim of bringing RealSense technology into shops is not to create databases of speci c peopleÕs lives but rather to build generalized models of peopleÕs lifestyles and shopping habits.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: ÒItÕs not so much that you need to know a particular customer.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: ItÕs that you need to know that this shopper has these characteristics and, in the past, that when those characteristics are present, this is what a person tends to do.Ó If you combine recognition technology with databases of previous customer patterns, you can start to predict a lot about what a person may or may not do in a shop.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: If, say, thereÕs a size-10 woman wearing a gold necklace walking quickly towards the sock aisle, you can use that data to predict she wants to, well, buy socks.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: That could allow a retailer to automatically put targeted ads on screens aimed speci cally at that person.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: If she looks like the type of person who wants to buy socks, they will show her adverts for socks.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: If it sounds familiar, itÕs because the online world has been using techniques like these for years.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: If you search for something on Amazon, youÕll be hounded by targeted banners for similar products on other sites.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: Express a vague interest in canoeing and youÕll get ads for canoes wherever you go.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: Yet bringing these systems into the physical world isnÕt a simple case of copy and paste.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: It turns out that people do not react to cameras in the same way as they do to browser cookies.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: Hoxton Analytics, a London-based team of data scientists, has developed a technology that makes use of machine learning and arti cial intelligence to categorize people based on the shoes they are wearing.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: By analysing the style and size of peopleÕs footwear as they walk past the sensor, the system can identify a customerÕs gender with between 75 and 80% accuracy.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: Owen McCormack, Hoxton Analytics CEO, says that the focus of the system came about in part as a reaction to facial recognition.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: ÒMy idea was, why donÕt we simply consider the clothes someoneÕs wearing to understand demographics?Ó he said.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: ÒIf I just showed you a shot of someoneÕs body you could probably tell me what gender they are.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: However, it turns out pointing a camera at someoneÕs chest or hips feels just as creepy as facial recognition.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: The idea was Ð what about peopleÕs shoes?Ó The word ÒcreepyÓ comes up a lot during discussions of in-store tracking.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: For retailers and data scientists, the aim is to nd a way of obtaining information without coming across as intrusive.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: For Hoxton Analytics and the retailers using the technology, the answer is to look downwards.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: This tactic of avoiding the face and staring at shoes says a lot about how we, as physical beings, react to being watched.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: It suggests there are boundaries that do not exist on the internet.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: Set sights on our torsos and we feel invaded.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: But is making calculated judgments about a person based on their footwear actually any less invasive?
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: For McCormack, the argument hinges on the fact that personally identi able information isnÕt being collected.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: ÒRight now, shops are doing lots of incredibly invasive things but we just donÕt know about it.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: The angle Hoxton Analytics is taking on that whole thing is, well, if you know someoneÕs a male or a female, then your advertising will be much more ef cient.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: If you know that everyone in your shop right now is a male, youÕll be advertising PlayStations not hairdryers.Ó Keep it hidden and invisible monitoring lets shops optimize their output while keeping the customer unaware.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: Put adaptable monitors and targeted advertising into the mix, however, and it becomes harder to hide the fact that a machine is watching you.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: The argument from the retailers is that they do this to provide a personal shopping experience but it remains a grey area.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: It still feels creepy.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: From the perspective of retailers, itÕs understandable that physical shops want some of the information online outlets collect.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: We allow this to happen online so why not of ine?
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: The thresholds of a shopping centre are different from those between websites and, when you can wander freely from one place to another without a pop-up asking you to accept cookies, the rules of consent change.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: Then again, for a generation growing up with online rst, physical shop second, the modes of online play may not be quite so invasive.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: In the CSC report, a survey indicated that while 72% of respondents aged 55 and over said they were very uncomfortable with these types of technologies being used in physical shops, only 51% of 16-24 year olds said the same.
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: Does this relative openness stem from a greater familiarity with digital technology or a blind belief in the goodwill of omnipresent organizations offering free services?
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: Is the creepiness of a technology an unvarying, instinctive certainty or does it ebb and ow with degrees of social acceptance?
label: ignore

title: item_p0081
complex: Whatever the case, there are a growing number of eyes between the shelves and they care a lot about what youÕre wearing.
label: ignore

title: item_p0082
complex: The brand and logo of Apple have been named the most valuable in the world – worth nearly $119bn, or more than the entire gross domestic product of Morocco, Ecuador or Oman.

title: item_p0082
complex: The Silicon Valley firm, already the world’s biggest company – with a stock market valuation of $591bn – has seen its brand value increase by 21% in 12 months, according to the closely followed Interbrand Best Global Brands annual report.
label: ignore

title: item_p0082
complex: Apple, which is recognized the world over by its simple “Apple with a bite missing” emblem, led a surge of technology companies in the 2014 report, which has pushed more traditionally valuable brands – such as Coca-Cola, McDonald’s and Gillette – down the table.
label: ignore

title: item_p0082
complex: Google’s brand value rose by 15% to $107bn to take second place, followed by Coca-Cola, up 3% to $81.5bn, IBM ($72.2bn) and Microsoft ($45.5bn).
label: ignore

title: item_p0082
complex: Facebook is the biggest riser in the chart, increasing its brand value by 86% to $14.3bn and taking 29th place in the table, ahead of longstanding global corporate names such as Volkswagen, Kellogg’s and Ford.
label: ignore

title: item_p0082
complex: Jez Frampton, chief executive of Interbrand, which is part of global advertising group Omnicom, said: “Benefitting immensely from the rise of digital and, later, mobile technology, savvy brands like Apple grew stronger.
label: ignore

title: item_p0082
complex: New categorykillers like Google, Amazon and Facebook have reset customer expectations and significantly raised the bar for brand experiences.” Apple, which former Chief Executive Steve Jobs founded in his Los Altos garage in 1976, only appeared in the top ten of the Interbrand annual study in 2011.Apple, which former Chief Executive Steve Jobs founded in his Los Altos garage in 1976, only appeared in the top ten of the Interbrand annual study in 2011.

title: item_p0082
complex: Its logo, created by advertising executive Rob Janoff in 1977, was designed with a bite taken out of it to avoid confusion with a cherry.
label: ignore

title: item_p0082
complex: “One of the deep mysteries to me is our logo, the symbol of lust and knowledge, bitten into, all crossed with the colours of the rainbow in the wrong order.
label: ignore

title: item_p0082
complex: You couldn’t dream of a more appropriate logo: lust, knowledge, hope and anarchy,” Janoff said.
label: ignore

title: item_p0082
complex: Graham Hayles, Interbrand’s chief marketing officer, said it was “not out of kilter” that Apple’s brand could account for a fifth of the company’s entire market value.
label: ignore

title: item_p0082
complex: “Apple makes a lot of money because it has a very strong brand,” he said.
label: ignore

title: item_p0082
complex: “There is a very strong correlation between branding and profitability.” Hayles said Interbrand, which has been carrying out the annual study since 2000, calculates brand value by examining companies’ financial performance, consumers’ “brand allegiance” and “brand-strength analysis”.
label: ignore

title: item_p0082
complex: While many technology companies rose up the chart, there were big fallers, too.

title: item_p0082
complex: Finnish mobilephone company Nokia dropped 41 places to 98th at $4.1bn, just ahead of Nintendo in 100th place (down 33).

title: item_p0082
complex: “They’re both only just in the chart now,” Hayles said.

title: item_p0082
complex: “It shows the importance of getting innovation right.
label: ignore

title: item_p0082
complex: If you don’t keep pace, it is very penalizing.” A Chinese company has made it into the top 100 for the first time, with mobile-phone and broadband firm Huawei entering the rankings in 94th place with a brand value of $4.3bn.

title: item_p0082
complex: Most of the brands in the top 100 are US-owned, the highest-placed non-US brands being South Korea’s Samsung (6th), Japan’s Toyota (8th) and Germany’s Mercedes-Benz (10th).

title: item_p0082
complex: The highestplaced British brands are HSBC (33rd), Shell (65th) and Burberry (73rd).

title: item_p0082
complex: Other fashion brands in the top 100 include Boss, Prada and Ralph Lauren.
label: ignore

title: item_p0082
complex: Designer label Louis Vuitton is the top-ranked fashion name, in 19th position, with a value of $23bn, just ahead of high-street clothing chain H&M, with a brand value of $21bn and ranked 21.

title: item_p0082
complex: Sports brand Nike, ranked 22 with a brand valued at nearly $20bn, is rated way ahead of rival Adidas, at 59 in the top 100 with a value of $7bn.

title: item_p0082
complex: Frampton said consumers’ ability to interact with and criticize brands on Twitter and other social media means companies must react faster to retain and improve their brands’ reputations.
label: ignore

title: item_p0082
complex: “The customer, empowered by social media in the ‘age of experience’, now has more control than ever,” he said.
label: ignore

title: item_p0082
complex: “In this world of two-way conversations, advocacy, influence and engagement are the new rules for brand-building.
label: ignore

title: item_p0082
complex: “Customers expect seamless interactions, responsiveness, 24/7 accessibility, customization options and high levels of personalization,” he said.

title: item_p0083
complex: Male bosses are being paid bonuses double the size of those given to female colleagues in identical jobs – a disparity that means men enjoy salary top-ups of £141,500 more than women over the course of a working lifetime.
label: ignore

title: item_p0083
complex: The figures, released by the Chartered Management Institute (CMI), reveal that men in UK management roles earned average bonuses of £6,442 in 2012 compared with £3,029 for women.
label: ignore

title: item_p0083
complex: In the most senior roles, female directors received bonuses of £36,270 over the past 12 months, compared with £63,700 awarded to male directors.
label: ignore

title: item_p0083
complex: The latest figures highlighting the inequitable nature of pay in British business led to calls for action from campaigners on workplace equality.
label: ignore

title: item_p0083
complex: Ann Francke, the CMI’s chief executive, said: “It’s time to move this issue into the mainstream management agenda.
label: ignore

title: item_p0083
complex: “This is about changing our approach to management to allow for greater flexibility, less masculine cultures, more emphasis on outcomes rather than time in the office and greater transparency around performance and rewards.
label: ignore

title: item_p0083
complex: “In solving this issue, we would actually raise the performance of organizations and the well-being of individuals at work.
label: ignore

title: item_p0083
complex: What are we waiting for?” Dr Ruth Sealy, a senior research fellow at Cranfield School of Management, added: “It is not surprising.
label: ignore

title: item_p0083
complex: Bonuses are a method of payment that can be used with discretion.
label: ignore

title: item_p0083
complex: As to what should be done about it, these things should be made more transparent.” While statisticians warned that some of the data may be skewed by factors such as women entering occupations where there is less of a culture of bonus payments, the discrepancies in the sizes of awards do appear to be aggravating Britain’s pay gap, which the government says is closing but still sees full-time male employees earn 10% more than women.
label: ignore

title: item_p0083
complex: Maria Miller, the Minister for Women and Equalities, said: “The CMI figures are yet another damaging example highlighting that, in the world of work, women still lose out to their male counterparts and that the playing field is far from level.
label: ignore

title: item_p0083
complex: “Changes in the workplace are happening and it’s good that the pay gap is closing – but there is still more to do before we see full equality in the workplace.
label: ignore

title: item_p0083
complex: “The government is playing its part: we have made pay secrecy clauses illegal, given tribunals the power to force employers who break equal pay laws to carry out equal pay audits and signed 120 companies up to our Think, Act, Report scheme, which encourages companies to improve the way they recruit, promote and pay women.
label: ignore

title: item_p0083
complex: “We’ve also looked at other pay gap causes, such as having to juggle work and family responsibilities, by introducing shared parental leave and the right to request flexible working to all employees.” Large companies such as Tesco, BT, Unilever and the international law firm Eversheds are among those signed up to Think, Act, Report.
label: ignore

title: item_p0083
complex: The scheme has only attracted 120 supporters in nearly two years of existence, having risen from 54 participants in November 2012.
label: ignore

title: item_p0083
complex: However, the CMI’s data did provide some evidence to support Miller’s contention that the overall pay gap is narrowing: the difference between the average salaries earned by male and female bosses has appeared to shrink, decreasing from an average of £10,060 in 2012 to £8,502 in the CMI’s most recent figures.
label: ignore

title: item_p0083
complex: However, the institute cautioned against direct comparisons between the 2012 and 2013 samples – which both polled around 40,000 managers – as they are not identical.
label: ignore

title: item_p0083
complex: A sub-set of 17,000 individual managers, whose salaries and bonuses have been tracked over a number of years, showed that male managers’ earnings are rising faster than women’s for the first time in five years, with men enjoying total increases of 3.2% compared with 2.8% for women, when salaries and bonuses are combined.
label: ignore

title: item_p0083
complex: At the most senior level, male directors’ earnings rose by 5.3% over the past 12 months, compared with just 1.1% for female directors.
label: ignore
